<TabItem label="JSX and TSX" icon="seti:javascript">
:::caution
This rule is part of the [nursery](/linter/#nursery) group. This means that it is experimental and the behavior can change at any time.
:::
## Summary
- Rule available since: `v2.4.13`
- Diagnostic Category: [`lint/nursery/noReactNativeRawText`](/reference/diagnostics#diagnostic-category)
- This rule doesn't have a fix.
- The default severity of this rule is [**error**](/reference/diagnostics#error).
- This rule belongs to the following domains:
  - [`reactNative`](/linter/domains#reactNative)
- Sources: 
  - Same as [`react-native/no-raw-text`](https://github.com/Intellicode/eslint-plugin-react-native/blob/master/docs/rules/no-raw-text.md)

## How to configure
```json title="biome.json"
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"nursery": {
				"noReactNativeRawText": "error"
			}
		}
	}
}

```
## Description
Disallow raw text outside `<Text>` components in React Native.

In React Native, every string rendered in the UI must be wrapped in a `<Text>`
component. Rendering text directly inside containers such as `<View>` throws at
runtime on native platforms.

By default, the following element names are treated as valid text containers:
`Text`, `TSpan`, `StyledText`, and `Animated.Text`. Additional components can be
whitelisted through the `skip` option.

## Examples

### Invalid

```jsx
<View>some text</View>
```

```jsx
<View>{'some text'}</View>
```

<View>{`${text}`}</View>

### Valid

```jsx
<View><Text>some text</Text></View>
```

```jsx
<View><Text>{'some text'}</Text></View>
```

## Options

### `skip`

An array of additional component names that are allowed to contain raw text.

```json title='biome.json'
{
	"linter": {
		"rules": {
			"nursery": {
				"noReactNativeRawText": {
					"options": {
						"skip": [
							"Title"
						]
					}
				}
			}
		}
	}
}

```

```jsx
const Title = ({ children }) => <Text>{children}</Text>;
<Title>This is the title</Title>;
```
